passages.content: Choose the best answer A, B, C or D to indicate the correct answer to the following questions.

questions.explanation: "Deliver" có nghĩa là giao hàng, phù hợp với ngữ cảnh gửi bưu kiện cho bà.
questions.text: I need to _________ this package to my grandmother next weekend.
The recipe needs two main _______ to make a delicious dish, including noodles and tomatoes.
questions.explanation: "Ingredients" là nguyên liệu, phù hợp với ngữ cảnh nấu ăn.

questions.text: The homework was so difficult that ________ students were forced to ask for help.
questions.explanation: "Students" là danh từ đếm được số nhiều, dùng "many" để chỉ số lượng lớn.

questions.text: The __________ in the race trained hard to win the first prize.
questions.explanation: "Competitors" là các vận động viên/người thi đấu, phù hợp với cuộc đua.

passages.title: Multiple Choice Questions
Social Communication

questions.explanation: Câu hỏi về kế hoạch cuối tuần sẽ dẫn đến câu trả lời khẳng định "Yes, I'm going to the park..."

options.B: Do you have any plans for the weekend?
options.C: I might go to the movies with my friends.

questions.text:
Mia: (5) _________________
Tom: Yes, I'm going to the park with my family.

Mia: (6) _________________
Tom: We'll have a picnic and play some games.

options.B: Do you have any plans for the weekend?
options.C: I might go to the movies with my friends.

questions.explanation: Sau khi biết Tom đi công viên, Mia nói "That sounds fun!" để thể hiện sự hào hứng.

questions.text:
Mia: Nice! (7) _________________
Tom: That sounds great! What movie are you going to see?

options.C: I might go to the movies with my friends.
options.B: Do you have any plans for the weekend?

questions.explanation: Câu trả lời của Tom hỏi về bộ phim, nên Mia phải nói về việc đi xem phim.

passages.content: Mia and Tom are talking with each other about weekend activities. Choose the best answer A, B, C or D to complete each gap in the conversation.
Read the following passage and choose the best answer A, B, C or D to indicate the correct word or phrase that best fits each of the numbered blanks.

questions.text: The bus (8) ________ was really fun, and we sang fun songs together.
questions.explanation: "Bus ride" là chuyến đi xe buýt, phù hợp với ngữ cảnh.

questions.text: When we arrived at the museum, we saw a lot of interesting (9) ________.
questions.explanation: Trong bảo tàng, người ta thấy các vật trưng bày (exhibits).

questions.text: My favourite was the dinosaur section because we were provided information about (10) ________ types of dinosaurs and their fossils.
questions.explanation: Cần tính từ "different" bổ nghĩa cho danh từ "types".
"Space" là danh từ không đếm được, không dùng mạo từ.
questions.text: We also watched a cool 3D movie about (11) ________ space.

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc "filled with" có nghĩa là tràn ngập.
questions.text: It was a great day (12) ________ with learning and laughter.

passages.title: Cloze Test

passages.content: Read the following passage and choose A, B, C or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions.

questions.explanation: Dòng đầu: "Raising a pet can be a fun and rewarding experience!" → mang lại niềm vui.
questions.text: What does the writer think of raising a pet?
According to the text, which of the following is NOT an example of a safe and comfortable home for a pet?
questions.explanation: Đoạn văn chỉ đề cập đến "a clean space, fresh water, and proper food" mà không nói về kích thước lồng.

questions.text: According to the writer, how can you get closer to your pets?
questions.explanation: Đoạn văn: "Spend time bonding with your pet. Play, cuddle, and train them to build a strong relationship."

options.C: Feed them only once a week
options.B: Take them to a doctor regularly
options.D: Leave them alone for long periods

questions.text: What does the word "them" refer to?
questions.explanation: "Them" trong câu "Play, cuddle, and train them" thay thế cho "pets".

questions.text: What does the writer want to say in the last paragraph?

options.B: We should have a lot of pets.
options.D: Neglecting your pets will make them love you more.
options.A: We should be angry with our pets because they are disobedient.
options.C: With love and patience, your pet can bring joy to your home.

questions.explanation: Đoạn cuối: "with love and care, they can become your best friends. Remember, a happy pet means a happy home!"

questions.explanation: Câu bị động thì hiện tại đơn với chủ ngữ số nhiều "many young people" dùng "are" và động từ "attracted".

options.C: Do many young people attracted by the show?
options.A: Is many young people attracted by the show?
options.D: Are many young people attract by the show?
options.B: Are many young people attracted by the show?

questions.text: Does the show attract many young people?

questions.explanation: "cartoon" có trọng âm rơi vào âm tiết thứ hai (/kɑːˈtuːn/), các từ còn lại có trọng âm rơi vào âm tiết thứ nhất.
questions.text: Identify the word that has a different stress pattern:
Identify the word that has a different stress pattern:
questions.explanation: "together" có trọng âm rơi vào âm tiết thứ hai (/təˈɡeðə(r)/), các từ còn lại có trọng âm rơi vào âm tiết thứ nhất.

passages.content: Choose the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the word which differs from the other three in the position of stress in each of the following questions.
Choose the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the following questions.

questions.text: Tim and Peter _______ STEAM lessons on Wednesdays.
questions.explanation: Chủ ngữ số nhiều "Tim and Peter" đi kèm với trạng từ chỉ tần suất "on Wednesdays" chỉ một thói quen ở hiện tại nên ta dùng thì Hiện tại đơn thể phủ định: "don't have".

questions.text: Would you like _________ sand castles with me?
questions.explanation: Cấu trúc đề nghị lịch sự: "Would you like + to-V" (Bạn có muốn làm gì đó cùng tôi không?).
Cụm danh từ đo lường: "a bar of chocolate" nghĩa là một thanh sô-cô-la.
questions.text: She gave him a _______ of chocolate as a birthday gift.

Jane: "_______ you wear uniform to school every day?"
Harry: “No, we needn’t.”
questions.explanation: Câu trả lời là "No, we needn't" (Không cần thiết) nên câu hỏi phù hợp nhất là sử dụng động từ khuyết thiếu bắt buộc "Must" (Có phải bắt buộc...?).

questions.text: Ann was absent from class yesterday because she _______ sick.
questions.explanation: Trạng từ thời gian "yesterday" chỉ quá khứ đơn, vì vậy động từ "get" được chuyển thành dạng quá khứ "got" (got sick: bị ốm).

questions.text: Mount Everest is the highest _______ the world.
questions.explanation: Sử dụng giới từ "in" đi kèm với "the world" để chỉ phạm vi không gian "trên thế giới".

questions.text: Lucy doesn’t often want much sugar in her coffee, just _______.
questions.explanation: "sugar" là danh từ không đếm được. Cụm từ chỉ lượng nhỏ phù hợp là "a little" (một chút).

questions.text:
– _______ do you collect stamps or other things?
– Yes, I’m a stamp _______.
questions.explanation: Ở vị trí trống thứ hai cần một danh từ chỉ người sau danh từ bổ nghĩa "stamp". "stamp collector" có nghĩa là người sưu tập tem.
Cụm động từ "look up" có nghĩa là tra cứu từ điển/thông tin.
questions.text: If you don’t know what a word means, try to guess the meaning, or ________ the word in the dictionary.

questions.explanation: Dựa vào ngữ cảnh "spend the whole day" (dành cả ngày) thì địa điểm vui chơi giải trí phù hợp nhất là "shopping mall" (trung tâm thương mại).
questions.text:
– Where do you usually meet your friends?
– We often spend the whole day in the ________.

passages.title: Multiple Choice Questions
Error Correction
passages.content: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the underlined part that needs correction in each of the following questions.

questions.explanation: "lately" có nghĩa là "gần đây", trong khi ngữ cảnh cần trạng từ chỉ cách thức "muộn" là "late" để đối lập với "early".
questions.text: They <u>often</u> (A) go to bed <u>early</u> (B) and <u>never</u> (C) get up <u>lately</u> (D).

questions.explanation: Danh từ số nhiều của "woman" là "women" (bất quy tắc), không thêm "s" thành "womens".
questions.text: Do you know <u>how many</u> (A) <u>womens</u> (B) <u>there are</u> (C) in <u>the</u> (D) living room?

passages.title: Closest Meaning
passages.content: Mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the word(s) CLOSEST in meaning to the underlined word(s) in each of the following sentences.

questions.explanation: Từ "trash" đồng nghĩa với "rubbish" (rác thải).
questions.text: I think we shouldn’t throw <u>trash</u> into the river.

questions.explanation: Từ liên kết "Finally" (cuối cùng) đồng nghĩa với "Lastly".
questions.text: <u>Finally</u>, we all agreed to go camping in the countryside.

passages.title: Opposite Meaning
passages.content: Mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the word(s) OPPOSITE in meaning to the underlined word(s) in each of the following sentences.

questions.explanation: Từ trái nghĩa với "lazy" (lười biếng) là "hard-working" (chăm chỉ).
questions.text: Felix usually gets bad marks because he’s very <u>lazy</u>.
<u>Turn off</u> the tap when we brush our teeth and wash the dishes to save water.
questions.explanation: Cụm động từ trái nghĩa với "turn off" (tắt) là "turn on" (bật).
Câu hỏi "What is someone like?" dùng để hỏi về tính cách, tính tình của ai đó. Câu trả lời phù hợp nhất là C "He’s talkative and funny" (Cậu ấy nói nhiều và hài hước).

options.D: He has blonde hair and blue eyes
options.B: He’d like a cup of coffee

questions.text: What’s your younger brother like?
Jerry: I think we should use less paper in order to save trees in the forest.

options.C: It’s nice of you to say so

questions.explanation: Để đồng tình với ý kiến, sáng kiến tích cực của người khác, ta dùng lời đáp "That's a good idea" (Đó là một ý kiến hay).

passages.content: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the correct response to each of the following exchanges.
passages.title: Social Communication

options.C: They will next Sunday take the kids to cinema.
options.A: They will take the kids to the cinema next Sunday.
options.D: The kids will take them to the cinema next Sunday.
options.B: Next Sunday they take will the kids to the cinema.

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc câu khẳng định thì Tương lai đơn: S + will + V + O + Trạng ngữ chỉ thời gian. "They will take the kids to the cinema next Sunday."
questions.text:
Reorder the given words to make correct sentences:
take / the kids / They / cinema / the / next / to / Sunday / will

options.B: He saw a big black beautiful car in the street last week.
options.D: Last week he saw a big beautiful black car in street.
options.A: He saw a beautiful big black car in the street last week.
options.C: He saw a black big beautiful car in the street last week.

questions.explanation: Quy tắc sắp xếp tính từ OpSACOMP: Opinion (beautiful) -> Size (big) -> Color (black). Trạng từ chỉ thời gian đặt cuối câu: "last week".
questions.text:
Reorder the given words to make correct sentences:
He / a / saw / in / big / car / black / the / beautiful / street / last week

Choose the sentence with the closest meaning to the original:
The woman couldn’t buy this flat because she was very poor.

options.D: Because of she was poor, she couldn't buy this flat.
options.B: The woman was so poor that she can't buy this flat.
options.C: The woman wasn't rich enough to buying this flat.
options.A: The woman was too poor to buy this flat.

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc "S + be + too + adj + to-V" (Quá... đến nỗi không thể làm gì) tương đương nghĩa với mệnh đề "because she was very poor".

options.A: How about decorating our house before Tet?
options.C: Shall we to decorate our house before Tet?
options.B: Why don't we decorating our house before Tet?
options.D: Would you like decorating our house before Tet?

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc gợi ý "Let's + V" tương đương cấu trúc "How about + V-ing?".
questions.text:
Choose the sentence with the closest meaning to the original:
Let’s decorate our house before Tet.

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc "S + be + so + adj + that + clause" tương đương với "It + be + such + a/an + adj + noun + that + clause".

options.D: The anime film was boring enough for me to fall asleep.
options.B: The anime film was too boring for me to fall asleep.
options.A: It was such a boring anime film that I fell asleep.
options.C: It was so boring anime film that I fell asleep.

questions.text:
Choose the sentence with the closest meaning to the original:
The anime film was so boring that I fell asleep.

options.A: Don't forget to clean the table after lunch time.
options.C: Don't forget clean the table after lunch time.
options.D: Do not forget to clean table after lunch time.
options.B: Not forget cleaning table after lunch time.

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc câu mệnh lệnh nhắc nhở: "Don't forget + to-V". Ta thêm mạo từ "the" trước danh từ xác định "table".
questions.text:
Make a correct sentence using the given clues:
not / forget / clean / table / after lunch time

options.A: When I was a child, I often made paper planes with my sister.
options.C: I was a child often make paper planes with my sister.
options.D: When I was a child, I often make paper planes for my sister.
options.B: As I am a child, I often make paper planes with my sister.

questions.explanation: Hành động xảy ra khi còn nhỏ "When I was a child" (quá khứ đơn), hành động lặp đi lặp lại trong quá khứ được chia ở quá khứ: "often made".
questions.text:
Make a correct sentence using the given clues:
often / I / a child / often / make / paper planes / my sister

Make a correct sentence using the given clues:
recycle / effective / solution / help / protect / environment

options.C: Recycling is a effective solution helping protect environment.
options.A: Recycling is an effective solution to help protect the environment.
options.D: To recycle are effective solutions helping protect the environment.
options.B: Recycle is effective solution to help protect environment.

questions.explanation: Sử dụng danh động từ "Recycling" làm chủ ngữ. "an effective solution" có mạo từ "an" do "effective" bắt đầu bằng nguyên âm. "solution + to-V" (giải pháp để làm gì đó).

Multiple Choice Questions
passages.content: Choose the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the following questions.

questions.text: I haven't got ___________ pets, but my friend has got a parrot.
questions.explanation: Trong câu phủ định, danh từ đếm được số nhiều "pets" đi với "any".

questions.text: Peggy wants ___________ a famous singer when she grows up.
questions.explanation: Cấu trúc "want + to-V" (muốn làm gì).

questions.text: The children ___________ with the sand on the beach now.
questions.explanation: Trạng từ "now" chỉ thời điểm hiện tại, chủ ngữ "the children" số nhiều nên động từ chia ở thì hiện tại tiếp diễn: "are playing".

questions.text: ___________ do you like cycling around the park every weekend? - Because it helps me to keep fit.
questions.explanation: Câu trả lời bắt đầu bằng "Because" (bởi vì) nên câu hỏi phải là "Why" (tại sao).

questions.text: Peter and his sister often go for a walk with their dog ___________ Sunday mornings.
questions.explanation: Dùng giới từ "on" với các buổi trong ngày khi có tính từ chỉ ngày cụ thể (Sunday mornings).

questions.text: I've lost my key. Could you help me to ___________ it?
questions.explanation: "look for" có nghĩa là tìm kiếm, phù hợp với ngữ cảnh bị mất chìa khóa.
Cần tính từ "sporty" (khỏe khoắn, năng động) để bổ nghĩa cho chủ ngữ "he".
questions.text: Nam plays soccer three times a week, so he looks very ___________.

questions.explanation: "raise your hand" là cụm từ cố định nghĩa là giơ tay (trong lớp học).
questions.text: Please ___________ your hand.
My cousin ___________ yoga.
questions.explanation: Động từ "do" đi với "yoga" (tập yoga).

questions.text: A ___________ is a large piece of furniture where you can hang your clothes.
questions.explanation: "wardrobe" là tủ quần áo, nơi treo quần áo.

questions.explanation: "people" là danh từ đếm được số nhiều, cần dùng "many" thay vì "much".
questions.text: There <u>are</u> (A) so <u>much</u> (B) people in the room that I can't <u>move</u> (D).
We need 2 <u>loaves</u> (B) of bread and a <u>slice</u> (C) of milk for the picnic.
questions.explanation: "slice" dùng cho bánh mì, thịt,… Không dùng cho sữa. Phải nói "a carton/bottle of milk".

questions.text: Worried
questions.explanation: "Worried" có nghĩa là lo lắng, đồng nghĩa với "nervous" (bồn chồn, lo âu).

questions.text: drugstore
questions.explanation: "drugstore" là hiệu thuốc, đồng nghĩa với "pharmacy".

questions.explanation: Câu hỏi về cách đến một địa điểm, câu trả lời phù hợp nhất là chỉ đường hoặc phương tiện: "You can take the bus number 07."

passages.title: Social Communication

questions.text:

My father will take a bus to Ho Chi Minh city next Sunday.

options.D: spent too much time playing computer games.
options.B: to spend too much time playing computer games.
options.C: spending too much time playing computer games.

TAKE PART IN A MOCK INTERVIEW FOR A SUMMER JOB
Your friend and you are planning to apply for a summer job in Da Nang. Prepare!

questions.text:
Put the sentences (a-c) in the correct order, then fill in the blank to make a logical text.
a. Talking in person also builds stronger relationships and creates more trust.
b. These non-verbal signals make the message easier to understand and help avoid confusion.
c. Spending time together allows friends and family to feel closer and more connected.
questions.explanation: Câu b ('These non-verbal signals...') bắt đầu để liên kết với câu trước ('eye contact, body language...'). Sau đó câu a ('Talking in person also...') phát triển thêm ý. Câu c ('Spending time together...') làm rõ hơn cho ý mối quan hệ khăng khít ở câu a.

A lot of teenagers love music. It helps them express themselves and connect with others. Teens often listen to music through headphones or go to concerts with friends. However, (19) _______ good or bad effects on teen behaviour?
Some people believe that music has a couple of positive effects. It can make teens feel better when they are sad, and it helps them connect (20) _______ like the same music. Also, learning to play an instrument or write songs can make teens more creative and disciplined.
(21) _______, some people worry about the negative effects. Some songs with violent or bad words might influence teens to act in risky ways. Music (22) _______ teens’ hearing if they listen to it too loudly through headphones.
Parents (23) _______ an important role in helping teens make good choices about music. They (24) _______ talk to teens about the meaning of songs and help them avoid harmful music. With the right guidance, music can have a good influence on teenagers.

questions.text: Choose the option that best fits blank (19).
questions.explanation: 'music' là danh từ không đếm được (số ít), trong câu hỏi ta dùng trợ động từ 'does' và động từ nguyên thể 'have'.

Trật tự từ đúng của động từ khuyết thiếu và trạng từ là: Modal verb (can) + Adverb (also) + Verb (hurt).

passages.content:
Language is indispensable to humans. It helps us share our thoughts, express our emotions, and understand one another. Without language, we wouldn’t be able to build relationships or learn from others. Over time, languages have gradually developed, changing in sounds, grammar, and vocabulary. That’s why the way people speak today is often very different from how their grandparents spoke.
Around the world, there are thousands of languages. Some are spoken by millions of people, while others are used by only a small group. Sadly, many of these smaller languages are disappearing. When a language dies, we also lose part of a culture and history. That’s why many specialists are working hard to protect endangered languages before they are lost forever.
Not all languages are spoken with voices. Sign languages use hand movements and facial expressions to share meaning. They are used by people who are deaf or hard of hearing and are just as rich and meaningful as spoken ones.
Learning another language can also help you personally. It can boost your self-confidence, strengthen your brain and help you understand different cultures. In today’s global world, speaking more than one language can help you open doors in both life and work.

Shopping is an important part of everyday life. (37) _______. Some enjoy going to small local shops, while others prefer big supermarkets or modern shopping malls. questions.text:
Choose the correct sentence to complete the first half of the following text.
To save energy at school, students should practise a few simple habits. To begin with, try to avoid wasting energy during lessons and at break time. _______

options.D: This means turning off lights, fans, and electronic devices when they are not in use, especially when leaving the classroom.
options.B: In short, learners must stop using all electronic devices during their lessons and only read printed books to save the school’s electricity.
options.A: Otherwise, the school can save a lot of money on monthly electricity bills and help the earth become a greener place.
options.C: However, students need to keep the schoolyard completely clean by picking up trash and putting plastic bottles into the correct recycling bins.

questions.explanation: Câu tiếp theo cần giải thích rõ hơn về cách 'tránh lãng phí năng lượng trong giờ học và giờ ra chơi' (avoid wasting energy during lessons and at break time). Phương án D giải thích rõ điều này bằng cách liệt kê việc tắt đèn, quạt và thiết bị điện khi không sử dụng.

Trình tự logic của các từ nối: 'Next' (tiếp theo - c) -> 'Finally' (cuối cùng - b) -> câu kết luận tóm tắt 'By taking these simple actions...' (bằng việc thực hiện những hành động đơn giản này... - a). Do đó thứ tự đúng là c – b – a.
questions.text:
Reorder the following sentences to complete the second half of the text in a logical way.
a. By taking these simple actions, students can help reduce energy waste and protect the environment.
b. Finally, they should advise their classmates to save energy and work together to build good habits in the classroom.
c. Next, they should make use of sunlight and fresh air whenever possible by opening windows instead of using electric lights and air conditioners.

passages.content:
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D on your answer sheet to indicate the correct answer to each of the following questions.

For young people, planning a career now means more than choosing a job they like. They also need to understand how work is changing. In the past, a person might learn one set of skills and use it for many years. Today, however, technology and new customer needs are changing the way people work.
Automation is one clear example. When a machine can do a task faster, more accurately and at a lower cost, that task may become obsolete. This has happened to some simple jobs in ticket offices, factories and service counters. However, technology does not only take work away. It also creates new opportunities in fields such as online business, video editing, digital services and software development.
Because of these changes, workers need more than technical knowledge. They often have to collaborate with other people, share information, and solve problems together. A doctor may work with nurses and technicians to manage digital records, and a hairdresser may use an online booking app to arrange visits and communicate with customers before they arrive. Even jobs that seem practical or traditional may now require basic digital skills.
There is no single career path that suits every student. University may be the right choice for some, while vocational courses may help others prepare for skilled work sooner. To build a successful future, teenagers should develop useful skills, stay curious, and be prepared for further changes in the job market.

Mark the letter A, B, C, or D on your answer sheet to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks.

MANAGING YOUR TIME BETTER
Many teenagers feel stressed because they have too much schoolwork and too many activities after class. To study more effectively, students should build good time-management habits. One useful way is to make a clear timetable and write down important tasks. This helps students avoid forgetting homework or leaving everything until the last minute.
Students can also divide big tasks into smaller steps. For example, instead of trying to finish a whole project in one evening, they can complete one part each day. This makes the work less difficult and gives students more confidence. (9)_______ can also help them stay calm before exams.
Another useful habit is to keep away from distractions while studying. Students should turn off unnecessary notifications and choose a quiet place to work. This (10)_______ their lessons more carefully and finish their homework faster.
Good time management is not only about studying. Teenagers also need time for rest, exercise and family. If they plan their day well, they can have enough time to relax without feeling guilty. This can improve both their physical and mental health. It may also help them (11)_______ better results at school.
However, students should not make their timetable too strict. A good plan should be realistic and flexible. When something unexpected happens, they can change their schedule instead of feeling stressed. Learning to manage time well is an important skill because it has a strong (12)_______ students’ future success.
(13)_______ students practise these habits regularly, they will become more independent and responsible. They will understand that (14)_______ is not only about working hard, but also about working wisely.

Four phrases/sentences have been removed from the text below. For each question, mark the letter A, B, C, or D on your answer sheet to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks.

PRESERVING TRADITIONS IN MODERN LIFE
Many young people today live in a fast-changing world. They use smartphones, study online and enjoy modern forms of entertainment. However, (37)_______. Many schools and families are finding creative ways to teach teenagers about cultural heritage. For example, many families still prepare traditional dishes during festivals and special occasions. Grandparents often teach children how to make spring rolls, sticky rice or other local dishes. These activities (38)_______ and understand the value of family traditions more clearly.
Traditional games are also worth preserving. In the past, (39)_______. Today, some schools organize Cultural Days, where students can try these games and learn about their meanings. Digital tools can also be used to introduce traditional music, costumes and festivals to young people through online videos and digital stories.
Preserving traditions does not mean refusing modern life. Instead, it means choosing useful ways to keep cultural values alive. Young people can share photos of family festivals online, interview older family members or learn traditional songs from their grandparents. They can also join local cultural events (40)_______. In conclusion, traditions help young people understand who they are and where they come from. When traditions and modern life go together, culture can continue to grow in meaningful ways.

Options:
A. to understand traditions better
B. children often played folk games in the village yard or school playground
C. help them learn practical skills
D. traditional customs still play an important role in family and community life

Ann: There’s a new pupil in our class. Her name’s Lily.
Tom: What’s she like?
questions.explanation: Câu hỏi 'What is she like?' dùng để hỏi về tính cách, tính chất của một người, do đó đáp án 'She's friendly' (Cô ấy thân thiện) là phù hợp nhất.
Khi ai đó đề nghị giúp đỡ bằng cấu trúc 'Do you want me to...', câu trả lời lịch sự để tiếp nhận sự giúp đỡ là 'That would be great!' (Thế thì tuyệt quá!).

passages.title: Part 3: Signs and Notices
Part 4: Reading Comprehension
passages.content:
Read the text below and circle the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each question.

There are many ways we can help protect the environment. First, we should try to recycle things. This means using old materials to make something new. We can recycle paper, glass, and plastic. By doing this, we save resources and reduce waste. Secondly, saving energy is essential. We can switch off lights when we leave a room and unplug chargers when they are not in use. This helps to reduce the amount of electricity we use. Additionally, we should try to close the tap while brushing our teeth can make a big difference. Finally, planting trees is a great way to help the environment. Trees clean the air and provide homes for animals. Everyone can do something small, and together we can make a big chance to help our planet.

questions.explanation: Đoạn văn liệt kê nhiều giải pháp khác nhau để bảo vệ môi trường như tái chế, tiết kiệm năng lượng, tiết kiệm nước và trồng cây. Ý bao quát nhất chính là câu A.

options.A: We can help to protect the environment in many ways.
options.C: Saving energy is the most important action to save the environment.
options.B: Recycling is a way to protect the planet.
options.D: Planting trees makes a big difference to our planet.

questions.text: What is the main idea of the passage?

passages.title: Part 5: Reading Story and Writing Tasks
passages.content:
Read the following story and do the following task.

I’m Mark and my little sister’s name is Katy. Last Saturday, we went to London with our parents. Mum drove us there in the car. Our cousin Michael came to London too, but he went by train, so we met him at King’s Cross Station. He had a heavy rucksack with him. “Put your rucksack in our car, Michael,” Dad said. “We’re going to walk now.” “No, thanks. I might need it,” Michael said.
We went to the dinosaur museum first. I asked Dad lots of questions, but he didn’t know the answers. Michael took a book about dinosaurs out of his rucksack. “Here you are!” he said to Dad. “Thanks, Michael,” said Dad. When we left the museum, it was raining. “It’s OK,” said Michael. I’ve got an umbrella in my rucksack.”
In the afternoon, we wanted to go to the cinema, but we couldn’t find it. Michael took a map of London out of his rucksack, and we soon found the street where the cinema was.

questions.text: Last Saturday, Mark, his sister Katy, and their parents went to London by _____.
questions.explanation: Dựa vào thông tin: 'Mum drove us there in the car.' Do đó phương tiện họ dùng là ô tô (car).

questions.text: At the dinosaur museum, Michael gave Mark’s dad a _____, and he could find the information he needed about the animals.
questions.explanation: Dựa vào thông tin: 'Michael took a book about dinosaurs out of his rucksack. “Here you are!” he said to Dad.'
Dựa vào chi tiết cuối câu chuyện: 'Michael took a map of London out of his rucksack, and we soon found the street where the cinema was.'
questions.text: Later, when they couldn’t find the cinema, Michael used a _____ from his rucksack to locate it.

options.C: to go to the movies tonight

Dear Sir/Madam,
a. I am writing to ask for information about the upcoming field trip to General Vo Nguyen Giap's Tomb.

passages.content:
Read the following passage about and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer to each of the following questions from 18 to 25.

James Watt was a Scottish inventor who played an important role in the Industrial Revolution. He was born in 1736 and is famous for improving the steam engine, which changed industries and transportation.
Watt loved mechanics from a young age. Even though he faced health problems and had little money, he stayed curious and hardworking. In 1763, while fixing a steam engine, he had an important idea. He realized that adding a separate part called a condenser could make the steam engine work better. This breakthrough was the start of his great inventions.
With the help of Matthew Boulton, a businessman, Watt developed his improved steam engine. In 1769, he got permission to make and sell it. His steam engine was very useful in industries like mining, textile making, and transportation. It helped factories, mills, and ships work faster and cheaper, making it an important part of the Industrial Revolution.
Watt didn't stop improving his steam engine. He made it safer, stronger, and cheaper to use. Because of his steam engine, production in factories became faster, and goods became cheaper. Many people moved from villages to cities to work in factories powered by steam. Steam-powered trains and ships also made travel and transporting goods easier.
James Watt's work is still remembered today. The unit of power, the watt, was named in his honour, recognising his pioneering contributions to science and engineering. He overcame many problems to make important inventions and inspire future generations.

passages.title: GLOBAL WARMING
passages.content:
Read the following passage about and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer to each of the following questions from 26 to 35.

Global warming is one of the most pressing issues of our time, caused by a rapid increase in greenhouse gases in the Earth's atmosphere. (I) Human activities, particularly the burning of fossil fuels like coal, oil, and gas, release large amounts of carbon dioxide (CO2) into the air. (II) These gases trap heat from the sun, creating a "greenhouse effect" that warms the planet. (III) While the Earth's climate has changed throughout history, the current rate of warming is unprecedented and poses serious challenges. (IV)
The impacts of global warming are far-reaching. Rising temperatures are causing polar ice caps and glaciers to melt, leading to higher sea levels. This puts coastal cities and low-lying areas at risk of flooding. Additionally, warmer oceans can intensify hurricanes and other extreme weather events. Changes in temperature and rainfall patterns disrupt ecosystems, threatening wildlife and leading to biodiversity loss. Global warming also affects agriculture, with heatwaves and droughts making it harder to grow crops in some regions.
Addressing global warming requires urgent action. Reducing greenhouse gas emissions is key to decelerating climate change. Governments, industries, and individuals all have roles to play. Transitioning to renewable energy sources like solar and wind power can significantly cut emissions. Protecting forests, which absorb CO2, and investing in energy-efficient technologies are also critical. By working together and adopting sustainable practices, we can mitigate the effects of global warming and secure a healthier future for our planet.

questions.explanation: Câu cần chèn giải thích rõ và kết nối với câu "These gases trap heat..." ở câu sau. Vị trí phù hợp nhất là (II).
questions.text:

"Scientists have long warned about the dangers of increasing greenhouse gas concentrations."

questions.explanation: Từ "far-reaching" (ảnh hưởng sâu rộng, nghiêm trọng) đồng nghĩa nhất với "serious".

questions.explanation: Xem đáp án chính thức từ đề thi: "This" ở đầu câu chỉ sự nóng lên toàn cầu (global warming) hoặc hiện tượng biến đổi khí hậu nói chung.

passages.content:
Read the passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer from 1 to 8.

Social media has significantly changed how different generations interact within a family. In the past, children might wait for a weekly phone call, but today, a relative can simply send a friend request. However, this digital connection can sometimes be awkward. One teenager recently complained that his grandma tagged him in a series of embarrassing childhood photos and shared them with all his school friends. He felt confused about whether he should accept the invitation or just defriend her to protect his privacy. Some parents even poke their nose in their children's online conversations, which often leads to heated arguments. This is a classic case of a misunderstanding where elders believe they are being supportive, while youngsters feel their personal space is being invaded. To bridge this gap, both sides need to have an honest word with each other about digital boundaries.

questions.explanation: 'Request' có nghĩa là lời yêu cầu, hành động hỏi xin.

passages.title: Sentence Arrangement

questions.text:
a. I realized that my previous attitude toward my elders was quite disrespectful.
b. Instead of ignoring my grandfather's advice, I decided to listen and reflect.
c. This change was indeed rewarding and made me feel much closer to him.
d. Living with my grandparents encouraged me to value their life experiences.
e. At first, it was hard to accept their strict rules and traditional views.
questions.explanation: d (sống với ông bà khuyến khích trân trọng) → e (ban đầu khó chấp nhận) → b (thay vì bỏ qua, lắng nghe) → a (nhận ra thái độ trước đây thiếu tôn trọng) → c (thay đổi đáng giá).
c (con trai đề nghị nói về quyền riêng tư trực tuyến) → d (bố đồng ý) → a (con bày tỏ cảm giác) → b (bố giải thích lý do) → e (con cảm ơn).
questions.text:
a. Son: I understand, but I feel like you're always checking my phone.
b. Father: That's because I'm worried about your safety online!
c. Son: Can we talk about my online privacy this weekend?
d. Father: Absolutely. A healthy relationship is based on trust and communication.
e. Son: Thanks, Dad. I really appreciate you listening to me.

a. Daughter: Then, text me the address of that family counselor.
b. Mother: It's lunch time. Would you like to join me for a talk?
c. Daughter: Thanks, but I'm going to meet my mates first and then I'll come home.
questions.explanation: b (mẹ rủ nói chuyện) → c (con cảm ơn nhưng từ chối) → a (con nhờ mẹ nhắn địa chỉ).

questions.text:

questions.explanation: c (cung cấp hướng dẫn) → b (được hội đồng trường chấp thuận) → e (cần xuất trình thẻ) → a (đăng ký đã được xử lý) → d (liên hệ nếu cần).

questions.text:
a. Những thay đổi này cho thấy sự tập trung rõ rệt vào phúc lợi của các gia đình.
b. Sự chuyển dịch này được minh chứng thêm bởi sự gia tăng của các căn hộ nhỏ cho người già.

questions.explanation: e (câu mở đầu về sự thay đổi cấu trúc hộ gia đình) → c (thay đổi về khu dân cư với trung tâm cộng đồng) → d (mở rộng dịch vụ hỗ trợ gia đình) → b (gia tăng căn hộ nhỏ cho người già) → a (kết luận về tập trung phúc lợi gia đình).

passages.content: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the best arrangement of sentences to make a cohesive text from 15 to 19.

Read the passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer from 20 to 29.

When we think about our grandparents' lives, we often look at the past through rose-tinted spectacles. We imagine a time of total peace and quiet where everyone was on good terms. However, the reality for the previous generations was often far more complex than we assume.

Many children in the mid-20th century were brought up in environments with very strict rules. While today we often discuss 'broken homes' as a modern crisis, serious family feuds have always existed, sometimes lasting for decades. The 'generation gap' was perhaps even wider then, as children were often expected to respect their elders' authority without any question.

Today, many of us take for granted the freedom to choose our own careers and partners. In the past, many young people felt they had no choice but to follow their parents' footsteps. While modern technology has created new types of friction, it has also given us tools to stay in touch more easily. To maintain a steady relationship, both generations must learn to appreciate the differences in their upbringing.

questions.explanation: Thành ngữ 'rose-tinted spectacles' chỉ việc nhìn nhận quá khứ một cách màu hồng, lạc quan thái quá.
questions.text: According to paragraph 1, our view of the past is often _______.
The word 'strict' in paragraph 2 mostly means _______.
questions.explanation: 'Strict' có nghĩa là nghiêm khắc, đòi hỏi tuân thủ quy tắc.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as a past issue?
questions.explanation: Nghiện kỹ thuật số (digital addiction) là vấn đề hiện đại, không được đề cập trong quá khứ.

questions.text: The phrase 'rose-tinted spectacles' describes a way of seeing things as _______.
questions.explanation: 'Rose-tinted spectacles' nghĩa là nhìn mọi thứ tốt đẹp hơn thực tế.
Câu: 'While modern technology has created new types of friction, it has also given us tools...' – 'it' thay thế cho 'modern technology'.
questions.text: The word 'it' in paragraph 3 refers to _______.
The phrase 'take for granted' in paragraph 3 refers to _______.
questions.explanation: 'Take for granted' có nghĩa là coi điều gì đó là hiển nhiên, không trân trọng.

options.B: failing to appreciate something because it is familiar

questions.text: Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3?
questions.explanation: Câu gốc: 'To maintain a steady relationship, both generations must learn to appreciate the differences in their upbringing.' – duy trì mối quan hệ bền vững đòi hỏi hiểu và trân trọng sự khác biệt trong cách nuôi dạy.

options.B: Both generations should live in the same house to be happy.
options.D: Upbringing is less important than technology in modern life.
options.A: Stability in a family requires understanding the different ways people were raised.
options.C: Learning about history is the only way to save a marriage.

questions.text: Which of the following can be inferred from the passage?

options.B: Family conflicts and the desire for independence are not new phenomena.
options.D: Grandparents never had to face any strict rules when they were young.
options.A: The past was much simpler and happier than the present.
options.C: Technology has made the generation gap impossible to close.

questions.explanation: Đoạn 2 nói về các cuộc cãi vã gia đình và khoảng cách thế hệ đã tồn tại từ lâu, không phải mới.
Câu này nên được đặt sau 'serious family feuds' (mâu thuẫn gia đình nghiêm trọng) vì nó giải thích hậu quả của những mâu thuẫn đó.
questions.text: Where in paragraph 2 does the following sentence best fit? 'These conflicts could split relatives apart for many years.'

options.C: A guide on how to use social media to contact grandparents.
options.A: The dangers of modern technology for children.
options.D: The history of the Stone Age and the Middle Ages.
options.B: A comparison of past and present family dynamics and the myth of a perfect past.

questions.explanation: Bài đọc so sánh động lực gia đình trong quá khứ và hiện tại, đồng thời phủ nhận quan điểm cho rằng quá khứ hoàn hảo.

passages.title: Multiple Choice Questions

questions.text: She always _______ whenever we see her.
questions.explanation: Chủ ngữ "She" số ít, thì hiện tại đơn diễn tả thói quen, động từ thêm "s". Ngoài ra, trạng từ "always" là dấu hiệu của thì hiện tại đơn.
"Packets" là danh từ đếm được số nhiều, dùng "many" để chỉ số lượng lớn trong câu khẳng định.
questions.text: We need to buy __________ packets of snacks for the picnic.
Mandy accidentally stepped on a snail in ______ school garden.
questions.explanation: Dùng mạo từ xác định "the" vì vườn trường là danh từ xác định.

questions.text: My friend________ the storytelling competition last Tuesday.
questions.explanation: Trạng từ chỉ thời gian "last Tuesday" là dấu hiệu của thì quá khứ đơn. Động từ "win" chia "won".
Trạng từ "now" chỉ thì hiện tại tiếp diễn: S + am/is/are + V-ing.
questions.text: Do not disturb him. He _______ a book now.
The teacher praised Luke_____ he did very well in his test.
questions.explanation: "Because" dùng để chỉ lý do (vì).

questions.text: Trucks are not allowed to pass through this _______ road.
questions.explanation: "Narrow" (hẹp) phù hợp với ngữ cảnh xe tải không được phép qua.

questions.text: Among three of them, Kelly is _____________.
questions.explanation: So sánh nhất với tính từ ngắn: the + adj-est.
Dùng giới từ "at" với địa điểm cụ thể như trạm xe buýt.
questions.text: Shall we wait for the bus________ the bus stop?

questions.explanation: Bác sĩ thường làm việc tại phòng khám (clinic).

questions.text: She never goes out late in the evening because she is <u>scared of</u> ghosts.

questions.text: When the doctor saw Ryan, he (23) ______ that he had not only a cough and fever, but also red spots...

passages.content: Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions.

questions.text: Ah Seng worked in the _________.
questions.explanation: Dòng đầu tiên của đoạn văn: "He was a shop assistant in a grocery store near the market."
Đoạn văn: "He was supposed to be the first to arrive and open the shop at six in the morning."

questions.explanation: Đoạn văn: "the five metal racks of dried goods, bread and cookies".
questions.text: What were on the five metal racks?
The grocery store was opened_________ days a week.
questions.explanation: Đoạn văn: "Ah Seng had to work every day except Sundays." → mở cửa 6 ngày/tuần.
Đoạn cuối: "He found it especially hard to wake up at five o’clock almost every morning and to follow the daily routine."

options.B: He did not want to work hard.
options.D: He found it difficult to follow the daily routine.
options.C: He did not like selling groceries.
options.A: He did not like his boss.

passages.content: Mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the sentence that is closest in meaning to each of the following questions or best combines each pair of sentences.

options.C: What is the high of Mount Everest?
options.A: What the height of Mount Everest is?
options.B: What is the height of Mount Everest?
options.D: What is height of Mount Everest?

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc câu hỏi về độ cao: "What is the height of + danh từ?"
questions.text: How high is Mount Everest?

options.B: I don't know as much about it as my sister did.
options.D: I don't know so much about it as my sister does.
options.A: I don't know much about it than my sister.
options.C: I don't know as much about it than my sister.

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc so sánh bằng dạng phủ định: S + don't/doesn't + V + so/as + much + as + S.
questions.text: My sister knows more about it than I do.
Their trip lasted three days.

options.A: They spent three days lasting the trip.
options.B: They lasted three days for their trip.
options.D: They last went on a trip three days ago.

questions.explanation: Cấu trúc viết lại với danh từ ghép: a three-day trip.
Liên từ "so" diễn tả kết quả: trời mưa nên hãy mang áo mưa hoặc ô.

options.C: It is going to rain, but you should bring a raincoat or an umbrella along with you.
options.A: It is going to rain, so you should bring a raincoat or an umbrella along with you.
options.D: It is going to rain, or you should bring a raincoat or an umbrella along with you.
options.B: It is going to rain, because you should bring a raincoat or an umbrella along with you.

questions.text: It is going to rain. You should bring a raincoat or an umbrella along with you.

options.B: If it will be sunny this weekend, we will go camping.
options.D: If it is sunny this weekend, we will go camping.
options.C: If it is sunny this weekend, we go camping.
options.A: If it may sunny this weekend, we will go camping then.

questions.explanation: Câu điều kiện loại 1: If + S + V (hiện tại đơn), S + will + V (nguyên thể).
questions.text: It may be sunny this weekend. We want to go camping then.
It was cold yesterday. We couldn't go swimming.
questions.explanation: Cấu trúc: S + be + too + adj + (for sb) + to-V.

options.C: It was too cold with us to go swimming yesterday.
options.A: It was too cold for us going swimming yesterday.
options.D: It was too cold with us going swimming yesterday.
options.B: It was too cold for us to go swimming yesterday.

options.D: She is reading the comic book under the light.

options.C: She is reading the comic book above the light.
options.A: She is reading a comic book on the light.

questions.text: She / read / comic book / light.
Sarah / not / come / party / if / not / invite.
questions.explanation: Câu điều kiện loại 1 dạng bị động: If + S + am/is/are + PII, S + will + V.

options.B: Sarah won't come to the party if she doesn't invite.
options.D: Sarah won't come to a party if she not invited.
options.A: Sarah won't come to the party if she isn't invited.
options.C: Sarah doesn't come to a party if she isn't invited to.

questions.text: Dear Customer Service Team, a. After bringing it home and installing it correctly, I found that it did not work properly. b. Please advise me on how to return the faulty item. I look forward to your prompt response. c. Whenever the power is on, the drum does not spin, and the machine makes a strange noise. d. I am writing to complain about a washing machine I purchased from your store on 22 February 2026. e. As this is a brand-new product and still under warranty, I would like to request a replacement or a full refund. Yours faithfully, Laura Brown

questions.text: When you see that something isn’t right, you should consider ___ immediate action to correct it.

passages.title: Reading Comprehension (My Grandmother Mary)
passages.content:
Read the passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer from 1 to 8.

My grandmother's name was Mary. She's dead now, but I remember her well. She was brought up on a farm in Wales, and her parents were very strict: as a teenager, they didn't allow her to listen to the radio or go to parties in the village. In the end, she decided to leave home and get a job in Cardiff. At first it wasn't easy, but she managed to find work, and she also met the man who became her husband.

My mother had a very different childhood. She grew up in the city, she was allowed to go to parties, and when she was in her teens, her parents let her stay out late. This shows how attitudes toward child-rearing have shifted over the decades. What one generation considers a necessity, the previous one might have viewed as a luxury or even a danger. Nowadays, the generation gap is often discussed in terms of technology use, but the fundamental issues of independence and rules remain the same.

questions.text: The phrase 'brought up' in paragraph 1 mostly means _______.
questions.explanation: 'Brought up' có nghĩa là được nuôi dưỡng, chăm sóc cho đến khi trưởng thành.
Đoạn 1 cho biết Mary bị cấm nghe radio và đi dự tiệc, nhưng không nói bà bị cấm tìm chồng. Thực tế bà đã gặp và lấy chồng ở Cardiff.
questions.text: Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as something Mary was forbidden from doing?

questions.explanation: 'Her' thay thế cho Mary – người được nhắc đến trong câu trước.
questions.text: The word 'her' in paragraph 1 refers to _______.

questions.explanation: Câu gốc: 'What one generation considers a necessity, the previous one might have viewed as a luxury or even a danger.' Nghĩa là quan điểm về điều cần thiết cho trẻ thay đổi qua các thế hệ.

options.C: Views on what children need change significantly from one generation to the next.
options.A: Every generation has identical views on what is necessary for children.
options.D: Previous generations were more successful at managing their households.
options.B: Parents and children always disagree on the dangers of modern life.

questions.text: Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2?
The phrase 'stay out late' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to _______.
questions.explanation: 'Stay out late' có nghĩa là ở ngoài đến tối muộn, về nhà sau khi trời tối.

questions.text: According to the passage, what is TRUE about the generation gap today?
questions.explanation: Đoạn 2 nói: 'Nowadays, the generation gap is often discussed in terms of technology use' – ngày nay, khoảng cách thế hệ thường được bàn luận dưới góc độ sử dụng công nghệ.

options.D: It only exists in rural areas like farms in Wales.
options.B: It is mainly caused by the difference in technology use.
options.C: It has eliminated all the rules for modern teenagers.
options.A: It is a completely new problem that didn't exist in the past.

questions.text: Which paragraph mentions the geographical shift from rural to urban living?
questions.explanation: Đoạn 1 kể Mary được nuôi dưỡng ở trang trại tại Wales (nông thôn), sau đó bỏ đi làm ở Cardiff (thành phố).
Đoạn 1 bắt đầu với câu: 'My grandmother's name was Mary. She's dead now, but I remember her well.' – đây là kỷ niệm tình cảm về người bà.
questions.text: Which paragraph discusses the emotional memories of a relative?

passages.title: Cloze Test (New Seminar on Family Relationships)
passages.content:
Read the following piece of news and mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 9 to 14.

New Seminar on Family Relationships
The city's social department is hosting a seminar featuring the largest (9) _______ of family experts in its history. The event aims to bridge the (10) _______ gap between parents and teenagers. Dr. Smith, a psychologist (11) _______ by the National Institute, will lead the discussion. He is among the (12) _______ experts in the field of adolescent behavior. The program promises a series of workshops, (13) _______ are hoped to help families (14) _______ stronger bonds and improve communication at home.

questions.text: The city's social department is hosting a seminar featuring the largest (9) _______ of family experts in its history.
questions.explanation: 'Number' dùng cho danh từ đếm được (experts). 'Volume' thường chỉ thể tích, 'amount' dùng cho không đếm được.

questions.text: The event aims to bridge the (10) _______ gap between parents and teenagers.
questions.explanation: Cần tính từ 'generational' (thuộc về thế hệ) bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'gap'.

questions.text: Dr. Smith, a psychologist (11) _______ by the National Institute, will lead the discussion.
questions.explanation: Rút gọn mệnh đề quan hệ dạng bị động: 'who is employed' -> 'employed'.

questions.text: He is among the (12) _______ experts in the field of adolescent behavior.
questions.explanation: 'Top experts' có nghĩa là các chuyên gia hàng đầu.
Đại từ quan hệ 'which' thay thế cho danh từ chỉ vật 'workshops'.
questions.text: The program promises a series of workshops, (13) _______ are hoped to help families (14) _______ stronger bonds.
... to help families (14) _______ stronger bonds and improve communication at home.
questions.explanation: 'Build up strong bonds' có nghĩa là xây dựng mối quan hệ gắn kết mạnh mẽ.

passages.title: Sentence Arrangement

questions.text:
a. I realized that my grandfather's strict rules were his way of showing care.
b. The reality, however, was that I often felt restricted and wanted more freedom.
c. This experience was indeed memorable and made me appreciate my family more.
d. Growing up in a multi-generational household encouraged me to value tradition.
e. Instead of rebelling, I started to have a word with him about my feelings.
questions.explanation: d (giới thiệu về gia đình đa thế hệ) → b (thực tế cảm thấy bị ràng buộc) → e (thay vì nổi loạn, trò chuyện) → a (nhận ra quy tắc là sự quan tâm) → c (kết luận trải nghiệm đáng nhớ).

questions.text:
a. Son: I understand, but I feel like I'm a casual acquaintance in this house sometimes.
b. Father: That's because you're always on your phone! We need some give and take.
c. Son: Can we talk about my curfew for the weekend?
d. Father: Absolutely. Family relationships are based on honest communication.
e. Son: Thanks, Dad. I really look up to you when you listen to me.
questions.explanation: c (con trai hỏi về giờ giới nghiêm) → d (bố đồng ý nói chuyện) → a (con bày tỏ cảm giác) → b (bố giải thích và đề nghị cho và nhận) → e (con cảm ơn).
b (mẹ đề nghị đưa đi) → c (con cảm ơn nhưng từ chối) → a (con nhờ mẹ nhắn địa chỉ quán cà phê).
questions.text:
a. Daughter: Then, text me the address of that new café.
b. Mother: It's getting late. Would you like me to give you a lift?
c. Daughter: Thanks, but I'm going to walk with my friends instead.

Dear Parents,
a. This session has been pre-approved by the school board for all families.
b. The invitation is exclusive and expires on Friday.
c. Your feedback will be processed, and a summary will be issued next week.
d. It is our honour to offer you a place in our 'Generation Bridge' workshop.
e. Should you require further details, please call our helpline.
questions.explanation: d (giới thiệu hội thảo) → a (được hội đồng trường chấp thuận) → c (phản hồi sẽ được xử lý) → b (thư mời chỉ có giá trị đến thứ Sáu) → e (liên hệ nếu cần thêm thông tin).
e (câu mở đầu về sự thay đổi cấu trúc hộ gia đình) → c (biến đổi về nhà ở) → d (đồng thời, người già dùng mạng xã hội nhiều hơn) → b (sự suy giảm gia đình hạt nhân) → a (kết luận về hiện đại hóa).
questions.text:
a. These developments demonstrate a clear modernisation of family living standards.
b. This shift was further evidenced by the decline of the traditional nuclear family.
c. Residential areas were noticeably transformed with the rise of smaller apartments.
d. Simultaneously, a significant expansion of social media use took place among the elderly.
e. Over the past decade, the structure of the average household has undergone a dramatic reshaping.

passages.content: Mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best arrangement to make a cohesive text from 15 to 19.

questions.text: According to paragraph 1, the 'generation gap' often results in _______.
questions.explanation: Đoạn 1: 'the generation gap becomes most visible, often manifesting in arguments over clothes, music, and social media habits.'

options.D: a complete redesign of the house

questions.explanation: 'Idolise' có nghĩa là ngưỡng mộ, tôn sùng (admire greatly).
questions.text: The word 'idolise' in paragraph 1 mostly means _______.
Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a cause of family arguments?
questions.explanation: Đoạn 1 liệt kê 'clothes, music, and social media habits' – không có 'food'.

questions.text: The word 'confused' in paragraph 2 describes a feeling of _______.
questions.explanation: 'Confused' có nghĩa là bối rối, không hiểu rõ.
Câu trước: 'While parents might feel confused by the rapid pace of digital change...' – 'This' chỉ 'modern technology' hoặc sự thay đổi kỹ thuật số. Phương án A phù hợp nhất.
questions.text: The word 'This' in paragraph 2 refers to _______.

questions.explanation: 'See eye to eye' là thành ngữ có nghĩa là đồng tình, có cùng quan điểm.
questions.text: The phrase 'see eye to eye' in paragraph 2 means _______.
Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3?

options.C: Screens are the only reason why relationships become unsteady.
options.A: It is necessary for families to interact directly to keep their bond strong.
options.D: Technology is vitally important for maintaining family contact.
options.B: Families should only use technology when they are together.

questions.explanation: Câu gốc: 'It is vitally important that families spend time together without screens' – nghĩa là các gia đình cần dành thời gian bên nhau không có màn hình thiết bị để duy trì mối quan hệ bền chặt.
Đoạn 3: 'If parents can learn to trust their children more, and if children can show more respect for their parents' experience, the gap can be narrowed.' → Sự tôn trọng và tin tưởng là chìa khóa.

options.C: Parents should never allow their children to have any privacy.
options.A: The generation gap is a modern invention caused only by the Internet.
options.D: Teenagers are usually more experienced than their parents in life.
options.B: Respect and trust are the keys to resolving family conflicts.

questions.text: Which of the following can be inferred from the passage?
Where in paragraph 2 does the following sentence best fit? 'Yet the underlying desire for mutual respect remains unchanged.'
questions.explanation: Câu này nên được đặt sau 'see eye to eye' (đồng tình), vì sau khi nói về khó khăn trong việc đồng thuận, câu này nhấn mạnh rằng mong muốn tôn trọng lẫn nhau vẫn không thay đổi.

questions.text: Which of the following best summarises the passage?

options.B: How to use social media to find new friends.
options.D: A guide to the best schools for teenagers.
options.A: The history of technology in the 21st century.
options.C: Understanding and narrowing the generation gap.

questions.explanation: Toàn bài nói về khoảng cách thế hệ, nguyên nhân (công nghệ, thiếu tôn trọng) và cách thu hẹp nó.

passages.content:
Read the passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the best answer from 20 to 29.

Relationships between parents and children change as they grow up. When children are young, they often idolise their parents, looking up to them for every decision. However, as they enter their teens, the desire for independence often leads to a rift in the family. This is the period when the 'generation gap' becomes most visible, often manifesting in arguments over clothes, music, and social media habits.

Modern technology has added a new layer to this old problem. While parents might feel confused by the rapid pace of digital change, teenagers often feel their parents are 'out of touch.' This discrepancy can make it difficult for both sides to see eye to eye. Parents may worry about the risks of the online world, while children feel their privacy is being invaded.

However, a healthy relationship needs a bit of give and take. If parents can learn to trust their children more, and if children can show more respect for their parents' experience, the gap can be narrowed. It is vitally important that families spend time together without screens to maintain a steady relationship.
passages.title: Reading Comprehension (Parents and Children)

passages.content:
Read the following leaflet and mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 30 to 35.

How to Bridge the Gap at Home?
Managing family relationships properly is essential for a stable life. (30) _______, many parents struggle with communication and give up, eventually leading (31) _______ a rift. Here are some tips for your home:

Listen to each (32) _______ carefully without interrupting.
Respect your children's (33) _______ space and privacy.
Practice a bit of (34) _______ and take in every discussion.
Prioritise things that bring lasting happiness to get your family’s (35) _______!

questions.text: Managing family relationships properly is essential for a stable life. (30) _______, many parents struggle with communication and give up, eventually leading (31) _______ a rift.
questions.explanation: Tương phản giữa tầm quan trọng (essential) và thực tế nhiều bậc cha mẹ gặp khó khăn → dùng 'However' (tuy nhiên).

questions.text: ... eventually leading (31) _______ a rift.
questions.explanation: Cụm từ 'lead to' có nghĩa là dẫn đến.
Cụm từ 'each other' (lẫn nhau). 'Listen to each other' = lắng nghe lẫn nhau.
questions.text: Listen to each (32) _______ carefully without interrupting.
Respect your children's (33) _______ space and privacy.
questions.explanation: 'Personal space' có nghĩa là không gian riêng tư của cá nhân.

questions.text: Practice a bit of (34) _______ and take in every discussion.
questions.explanation: Cụm từ 'give and take' có nghĩa là sự nhường nhịn, thỏa hiệp lẫn nhau.

questions.text: Prioritise things that bring lasting happiness to get your family's (35) _______!
questions.explanation: Cụm từ 'get your family's worth' mang nghĩa nhận được giá trị xứng đáng cho gia đình.

passages.title: Leaflet (How to Bridge the Gap at Home)
Cloze Test (Understanding the Generation Gap)
passages.content:
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 36 to 40.

The process of understanding the generation gap is inevitably complex. Some experts undertake a process of extensive research, (36) _______. Other parents with no patience to explore these complex theories (37) _______, for the sake of their convenience rather than trying to solve the problem. This is why many families continue to have the same old arguments (38) _______. (39) _______, small changes in communication can lead to significant improvements. For example, replacing digital messages with face-to-face talk (40) _______ can help both sides feel more connected.

questions.text: Some experts undertake a process of extensive research, (36) _______.
questions.explanation: Cấu trúc phù hợp để mô tả các nghiên cứu được đánh giá: 'each of which is evaluated...' – 'which' thay thế cho 'research'.

options.B: every one of them is ignored by modern teenagers
options.D: those with similarities to other social issues
options.A: each of which is evaluated in a psychological study
options.C: when it is similarly compared with other families

options.D: deliberately confine themselves to a large number of choices
options.B: being deliberately confined themselves to fewer choices
options.C: were deliberate to exclude their children's opinions
options.A: will deliberately confine themselves to a small number of rules

questions.explanation: Cần một động từ chính. 'will deliberately confine themselves to a small number of rules' phù hợp về nghĩa (tự giới hạn bản thân vào một số ít quy tắc) và ngữ pháp.
questions.text: Other parents with no patience to explore these complex theories (37) _______, for the sake of their convenience...
This is why many families continue to have the same old arguments (38) _______.
questions.explanation: Các gia đình tiếp tục cãi nhau 'dù biết tác hại lâu dài' – phù hợp với ngữ cảnh phê phán sự thiếu kiên nhẫn.

options.D: so that they can maintain their authority
options.B: because they are well aware of the benefits
options.C: although they have a very healthy relationship
options.A: despite knowing the long-term harm it causes

questions.explanation: Tương phản với thực tế tiêu cực ở câu trước: 'However' (tuy nhiên) dẫn dắt một ý tích cực.
questions.text: (39) _______, small changes in communication can lead to significant improvements.
... replacing digital messages with face-to-face talk (40) _______ can help both sides feel more connected.
questions.explanation: 'On a regular basis' có nghĩa là thường xuyên, đều đặn – phù hợp với lời khuyên thực tế.

exams.title: Đề thi thử tốt nghiêp THPT - Chủ đề Generation Gap 1
exams.examName: Đề thi thử tốt nghiêp THPT  - Cambridge Vocabulary (The Generation Gap)

passages.content: Sắp xếp các câu để tạo thành đoạn văn hoặc đoạn hội thoại hoàn chỉnh.

questions.text: Dear Customer Service Team, a. After bringing it home and installing it correctly, I found that it did not work properly. b. Please advise me on how to return the faulty item. I look forward to your prompt response. c. Whenever the power is on, the drum does not spin, and the machine makes a strange noise. d. I am writing to complain about a washing machine I purchased from your store on 22 February 2026. e. As this is a brand-new product and still under warranty, I would like to request a replacement or a full refund. Yours faithfully, Laura Brown
questions.explanation: Thứ tự hợp lý: d (giới thiệu lý do viết thư) → a (sau khi mua và cài đặt) → c (mô tả vấn đề) → e (yêu cầu thay thế/hoàn tiền) → b (đề nghị hướng dẫn trả hàng).

questions.text: a. The upward trend continued and in 2020, more than half of its population lived in urban areas. In the next twenty years, the urban population is expected to reach 65 per cent of the total population. b. The line graph shows population trends in Fantasia’s urban and rural areas over the 1950-2040 period. c. By contrast, the urban population rose throughout the same period. In 1950, the percentage of urban population was just around 6 per cent. It increased slightly to 15 per cent in 1980. d. Overall, the urban population has increased and will continue to grow while the rural population has decreased and will continue to fall. e. In 1950, 94 per cent or most of Fantasia’s population lived in rural areas; this figure remained stable until 1960 before falling to 48 per cent in 2020 and is expected to drop to 35 per cent in 2040.
questions.explanation: Thứ tự: b (giới thiệu biểu đồ) → d (nhận xét tổng quát) → e (số liệu dân số nông thôn) → c (số liệu dân số thành thị tương ứng) → a (xu hướng tăng tiếp tục và dự báo).
Thứ tự: c (Lucy hỏi về bài thi) → a (Mike trả lời, hỏi lại) → e (Lucy trả lời) → d (Mike rủ học bài) → b (Lucy đồng ý).
questions.text: a. Mike: Not bad, thanks. I’m just glad it’s over! How about you? How’d your presentation go? b. Lucy: Sure thing! Come over around 10:00, after breakfast. c. Lucy: Hey! How did your Pragmatics exam go? d. Mike: No problem. So… do you feel like studying tomorrow for our English exam? e. Lucy: Oh, it went really well. Thanks for helping me with it!!

a. Firstly, it helps people distinguish between reliable sources and misleading or false content. b. As a result, individuals are better equipped to make informed decisions and avoid being manipulated by misinformation. c. Media literacy also encourages audiences to recognize bias, persuasive techniques, and hidden agendas in advertisements and news reports. d. On a larger scale, integrating media literacy education into school curricula is crucial for developing responsible and critical thinkers. e. Media literacy is an essential skill in the modern world because it enables individuals to critically evaluate the vast amount of information they encounter every day.
questions.explanation: Thứ tự: e (định nghĩa và tầm quan trọng) → a (lợi ích đầu tiên) → c (lợi ích khác) → b (kết quả) → d (mở rộng quy mô).

questions.text: a. Katy: Honestly, I’m not sure if I’ll go. I don’t know Sam that well. b. Jane: Hi, Katy. Are you going to Sam’s party at the weekend? c. Jane: Don’t worry. He’s very friendly, so I think you two will get along.
questions.explanation: Thứ tự: b (Jane hỏi) → a (Katy trả lời băn khoăn) → c (Jane trấn an).

passages.title: Sentence Arrangement (1-5)

passages.content: Đọc đoạn văn và trả lời câu hỏi.

options.C: The body may completely exhaust its internal energy reserves
options.A: The brain requires additional energy during periods of stress
options.D: Carbohydrates serve as a rapid source of energy for the body

questions.explanation: Đoạn văn không nói cơ thể cạn kiệt hoàn toàn năng lượng dự trữ; mà nói não có thể phớt lờ tín hiệu từ cơ thể ngay cả khi cơ thể vẫn còn đủ dự trữ năng lượng.
questions.text: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a reason for sugary food cravings?

questions.explanation: 'Gravitate towards' có nghĩa là bị thu hút, có xu hướng tìm đến; trái nghĩa với 'abstain from' (kiêng, tránh xa).
questions.text: The phrase “gravitate towards” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to

questions.explanation: 'Registers' ở đây có nghĩa là nhận ra, ghi nhận, tương đương với 'perceives' (nhận thức).
questions.text: The word “registers” in paragraph 3 can be best replaced by
The word “they” in paragraph 6 refers to
questions.explanation: 'They' ở đây thay thế cho 'cravings for sweets' được đề cập trước đó.
Phương án A diễn đạt lại chính xác nội dung: khi não thiếu năng lượng → tăng hormone stress → tăng nguy cơ bệnh tim hoặc trầm cảm.

options.A: A sustained increase in stress hormones may arise when the brain lacks energy, eventually raising the risk of heart disease or depression
options.C: Insufficient food intake may prompt the brain to increase stress hormone production, thereby raising long-term risks of cardiovascular and mental health disorders
options.D: When people do not consume sufficient food, the body gradually struggles to regulate stress hormones, which may contribute to chronic illness
options.B: Skipping meals can unintentionally trigger serious conditions such as stroke or heart disease because the brain does not receive enough energy

questions.text: Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 5?
Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
